thread:
Mainstream coverage of psi research stops at 'fringe.'

The data does not.

Princeton's PEAR lab: 28 years, p < 10⁻¹⁰. CIA Stargate: 89.5% return rate. Ganzfeld meta-analysis: p < 10⁻⁹.

The numbers nobody quotes ↓

1979-2007. Princeton, basement of the engineering school.

The PEAR Lab ran ~1.7M experimental trials. ~1,100 subjects.

The per-trial effect: tiny (1 in 10,000 deviation from chance).

The cumulative significance: beyond p < 10⁻¹⁰.

One in ten billion is not noise.

2010. Storm, Tressoldi & Di Risio.

They aggregated 30 Ganzfeld telepathy studies in *Psychological Bulletin* — one of the highest-impact psychology journals.

Combined hit rate: 32.2%. Chance: 25%.

Significance: p < 10⁻⁹.

And then Wagenmakers came for them...

Wagenmakers re-analyzed the same data using Bayesian methods.

Opposite conclusion: no evidence of psi.

Storm rebutted: the Wagenmakers re-analysis depends on prior assumptions the data don't warrant.

This is what an unresolved methodological debate looks like in 2026.

The CIA Stargate program: 1972-1995. $20M of taxpayer money. 450+ missions. 19 intelligence agencies. 89.5% return rate.

Jessica Utts (UC Davis statistician, later President of the American Statistical Association) concluded the effect was real.

Then they closed it. Three months before the AIR evaluation results landed.

Why does mainstream rarely cite these numbers?

Because the framing question matters more than the data.

'Do you accept psi?' = metaphysical commitment.
'What does the data show?' = measurable empirical inquiry.

Mainstream picks the first framing. The data answers the second.

The honest investigator-lane position:

1. The cumulative significance is real.
2. The mechanism is unknown.
3. The reasonable position is curiosity — not belief, not dismissal.

If you came from priors saying 'the universe doesn't work this way,' update slightly. If you came from 'everything is suppressed truth,' also update.

I ran a 14-day self-experiment to test whether the PEAR-style intention effect shows up in everyday life under a strict protocol.

Result: 12/12 under my coding. 9/12 under stricter coding.

I publish both numbers. I publish the misses.

Field report: blackswanp.com/courses/the-alignment-protocol/preview

The full pillar with every primary source, every effect size, every methodological caveat:

blackswanp.com/research/phenomena/psi-research-evidence

This is what an investigator-lane review of psi research looks like in 2026. Not believer. Not skeptic. Investigator.
